The area of a triangle with vertices $(x_1,y_1)$, $(x_2,y_2)$, and $(x_3,y_3)$ is given by the formula
\[\frac{1}{2} \left| x_1(y_2 - y_3) + x_2(y_3 - y_1) + x_3(y_1 - y_2) \right|.\]In this case, the area is
\[\frac{1}{2} \left| \log_2 1 (\log_2 4 - \log_2 8) + \log_2 3 (\log_2 8 - \log_2 2) + \log_2 7 (\log_2 2 - \log_2 4) \right|.\]Simplifying, we get
\[\frac{1}{2} \left| 0 + \log_2 3 (3 - 1) + \log_2 7 (1 - 2) \right| = \frac{1}{2} \left| 2 \log_2 3 - \log_2 7 \right| = \frac{1}{2} \log_2 \frac{9}{7} = \boxed{\log_2 \frac{3}{\sqrt{7}}}.\]The answer is $\boxed{\textbf{(B)}}$. The polynomial $x^3 + ax^2 + bx + 6$ has three distinct integer roots if and only if it can be factored as $(x-r)(x-s)(x-t)$, where $r$, $s$, and $t$ are distinct integers. Expanding, we get $x^3 - (r+s+t)x^2 + (rs+st+rt)x - rst$. Thus, $a = -(r+s+t)$, $b = rs+st+rt$, and $rst = -6$. The possible values of $r$, $s$, and $t$ are $-1$, $-2$, $-3$, $-6$, $1$, $2$, $3$, and $6$. The only sets of three distinct integers whose product is $-6$ are $(-1, -2, 3)$ and $(-1, -3, 2)$. Thus, there are $2$ possible sets of roots. There are $\binom{10}{3} = 120$ possible sets of roots, so the probability is $\frac{2}{120} = \boxed{\frac{1}{60}}$. We have that
\begin{align*}
\frac{F_2}{F_1}+\frac{F_4}{F_2}+\frac{F_6}{F_3}+\cdots+\frac{F_{20}}{F_{10}}&=1+2+3+\cdots+10\\
&=\frac{10(10+1)}{2}\\
&=\boxed{55}.
\end{align*}We can also use the fact that $F_n=\frac{\phi^n-\psi^n}{\sqrt{5}}$ for $n\geq 1,$ where $\phi=\frac{1+\sqrt{5}}{2}$ and $\psi=\frac{1-\sqrt{5}}{2}.$ Then
\begin{align*}
\frac{F_2}{F_1}+\frac{F_4}{F_2}+\frac{F_6}{F_3}+\cdots+\frac{F_{20}}{F_{10}}&=\frac{F_2}{F_1}+\frac{F_4}{F_2}+\frac{F_6}{F_3}+\cdots+\frac{F_{20}}{F_{10}}\\
&=\frac{\phi-\psi}{\phi}+\frac{\phi^2-\psi^2}{\phi^2}+\frac{\phi^3-\psi^3}{\phi^3}+\cdots+\frac{\phi^{10}-\psi^{10}}{\phi^{10}}\\
